description.en: Build a complete deep learning project: data collection, preprocessing, model design, training, evaluation, optimization, and deployment-ready export.
description.ta: முழுமையான deep learning திட்டத்தை உருவாக்குங்கள்: தரவு சேகரிப்பு, முன்-செயலாக்கம், மாதிரி வடிவமைப்பு, பயிற்சி, மதிப்பீடு, உகப்பாக்கம், வரிசைப்படுத்தல்.

story_variants.en:
## Vaathiyaar's Final Challenge

Vaathiyaar claps his hands. "You've learned every piece. Now put it all together. A real project from raw data to a model someone can actually use. This is where theory becomes practice."

### Complete Project: Fashion Item Classifier

```python
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader, random_split
import torchvision
import torchvision.transforms as transforms

# ============ STEP 1: DATA ============
transform = transforms.Compose([
    transforms.RandomHorizontalFlip(),
    transforms.RandomRotation(10),
    transforms.ToTensor(),
    transforms.Normalize((0.2860,), (0.3530,))
])

transform_test = transforms.Compose([
    transforms.ToTensor(),
    transforms.Normalize((0.2860,), (0.3530,))
])

full_train = torchvision.datasets.FashionMNIST('./data', train=True, download=True, transform=transform)
test_set = torchvision.datasets.FashionMNIST('./data', train=False, download=True, transform=transform_test)

train_set, val_set = random_split(full_train, [50000, 10000])

train_loader = DataLoader(train_set, batch_size=128, shuffle=True, num_workers=2)
val_loader = DataLoader(val_set, batch_size=128)
test_loader = DataLoader(test_set, batch_size=128)

classes = ['T-shirt', 'Trouser', 'Pullover', 'Dress', 'Coat',
           'Sandal', 'Shirt', 'Sneaker', 'Bag', 'Ankle boot']

# ============ STEP 2: MODEL ============
class FashionCNN(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.features = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(1, 32, 3, padding=1), nn.BatchNorm2d(32), nn.ReLU(),
            nn.Conv2d(32, 32, 3, padding=1), nn.BatchNorm2d(32), nn.ReLU(),
            nn.MaxPool2d(2), nn.Dropout2d(0.25),
            
            nn.Conv2d(32, 64, 3, padding=1), nn.BatchNorm2d(64), nn.ReLU(),
            nn.Conv2d(64, 64, 3, padding=1), nn.BatchNorm2d(64), nn.ReLU(),
            nn.MaxPool2d(2), nn.Dropout2d(0.25),
        )
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(64 * 7 * 7, 256),
            nn.BatchNorm1d(256),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.Dropout(0.5),
            nn.Linear(256, 10)
        )
    
    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.features(x)
        x = x.view(x.size(0), -1)
        return self.classifier(x)

model = FashionCNN()

# ============ STEP 3: TRAINING ============
device = torch.device('cuda' if torch.cuda.is_available() else 'cpu')
model = model.to(device)

criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()
optimizer = torch.optim.Adam(model.parameters(), lr=0.001, weight_decay=1e-4)
scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.ReduceLROnPlateau(optimizer, patience=3, factor=0.5)

best_val_acc = 0
for epoch in range(30):
    # Train
    model.train()
    train_loss, train_correct, train_total = 0, 0, 0
    for images, labels in train_loader:
        images, labels = images.to(device), labels.to(device)
        optimizer.zero_grad()
        outputs = model(images)
        loss = criterion(outputs, labels)
        loss.backward()
        optimizer.step()
        train_loss += loss.item()
        train_correct += outputs.argmax(1).eq(labels).sum().item()
        train_total += labels.size(0)
    
    # Validate
    model.eval()
    val_loss, val_correct, val_total = 0, 0, 0
    with torch.no_grad():
        for images, labels in val_loader:
            images, labels = images.to(device), labels.to(device)
            outputs = model(images)
            val_loss += criterion(outputs, labels).item()
            val_correct += outputs.argmax(1).eq(labels).sum().item()
            val_total += labels.size(0)
    
    train_acc = 100. * train_correct / train_total
    val_acc = 100. * val_correct / val_total
    avg_val_loss = val_loss / len(val_loader)
    scheduler.step(avg_val_loss)
    
    if val_acc > best_val_acc:
        best_val_acc = val_acc
        torch.save(model.state_dict(), 'best_fashion_model.pth')
    
    print(f'Epoch {epoch}: Train Acc={train_acc:.1f}%, Val Acc={val_acc:.1f}%, LR={optimizer.param_groups[0]["lr"]:.6f}')

# ============ STEP 4: EVALUATION ============
model.load_state_dict(torch.load('best_fashion_model.pth'))
model.eval()

test_correct, test_total = 0, 0
class_correct = [0] * 10
class_total = [0] * 10

with torch.no_grad():
    for images, labels in test_loader:
        images, labels = images.to(device), labels.to(device)
        outputs = model(images)
        _, predicted = outputs.max(1)
        test_total += labels.size(0)
        test_correct += predicted.eq(labels).sum().item()
        for i in range(labels.size(0)):
            class_correct[labels[i]] += predicted[i].eq(labels[i]).item()
            class_total[labels[i]] += 1

print(f'\nTest Accuracy: {100.*test_correct/test_total:.2f}%')
for i in range(10):
    print(f'{classes[i]:>12}: {100.*class_correct[i]/class_total[i]:.1f}%')

# ============ STEP 5: OPTIMIZE & EXPORT ============
model_cpu = model.cpu()

# Quantize
quantized = torch.quantization.quantize_dynamic(model_cpu, {nn.Linear}, dtype=torch.qint8)

# Export ONNX
model_cpu.eval()
dummy = torch.randn(1, 1, 28, 28)
torch.onnx.export(model_cpu, dummy, 'fashion_classifier.onnx',
                  input_names=['image'], output_names=['prediction'],
                  dynamic_axes={'image': {0: 'batch'}, 'prediction': {0: 'batch'}})

print('Model exported to ONNX! Ready for deployment.')
```

> **Congratulations!** You've completed the entire deep learning journey — from a single perceptron to a production-ready classifier!
story_variants.ta:
## வாத்தியாரின் இறுதி சவால்

வாத்தியார் கைதட்டுகிறார். "நீங்கள் ஒவ்வொரு பகுதியையும் கற்றுக்கொண்டீர்கள். இப்போது அனைத்தையும் ஒன்றாக இணையுங்கள். கச்சா தரவிலிருந்து யாரோ ஒருவர் உண்மையில் பயன்படுத்தக்கூடிய மாதிரி வரை — ஒரு உண்மையான திட்டம்."

explanation.en: Load FashionMNIST, apply augmentation (flip, rotate), normalize, split train/val/test
Hold out 10K samples for validation. Never tune on test set!
CNN with BatchNorm, Dropout, two conv blocks + classifier. Production-quality architecture
Standard conv block pattern: Conv -> BN -> ReLU. Repeat throughout the network
Adam + weight decay + ReduceLROnPlateau scheduler + best model checkpointing
Reduce LR by 50% if val loss doesn't improve for 3 epochs. Smart LR annealing
Save only the best model. Load it for final evaluation, not the last epoch's model
Evaluate on held-out test set. Report overall AND per-class accuracy
Quantize for size, export to ONNX for cross-platform deployment
Final artifact: an ONNX model ready to serve in any production environment

left.label: Before This Course
left.content:
What is a neuron?
What is a tensor?
How does training work?

right.label: After This Course
right.content:
Built CNN from scratch
Trained on real data
Deployed production model
25 lessons mastered!

instruction.en: Extend the Fashion classifier project: 1) Add a confusion matrix visualization, 2) Implement inference function that takes a single image and returns top-3 predictions with confidence scores, 3) Add model versioning with timestamp.
instruction.ta: Fashion classifier திட்டத்தை விரிவாக்குங்கள்: 1) குழப்ப அணி, 2) ஒற்றை படத்திற்கு top-3 கணிப்புகள், 3) timestamp உடன் மாதிரி பதிப்பாக்கம்.

en: predict_single: unsqueeze(0) for batch dim, F.softmax(output, dim=1) for probs
torch.topk(probs, 3) returns top-3 values and indices
ta: predict_single: batch dim-க்கு unsqueeze(0), probs-க்கு F.softmax(output, dim=1)
torch.topk(probs, 3) top-3 மதிப்புகள் மற்றும் indices திருப்பி அனுப்புகிறது

q.en: What is the correct order of a deep learning project pipeline?
q.ta: Deep learning திட்ட குழாய்வழியின் சரியான வரிசை என்ன?

en: Data -> Model Design -> Train -> Evaluate -> Optimize -> Deploy
ta: Data -> Model Design -> Train -> Evaluate -> Optimize -> Deploy

q.en: Why should you never tune hyperparameters on the test set?
q.ta: Test set-ல் ஏன் ஒருபோதும் hyperparameters-ஐ tune செய்யக்கூடாது?

en: It causes data leakage — your test evaluation won't reflect real-world performance
The test set is too small
ta: தரவு கசிவை ஏற்படுத்துகிறது — உங்கள் test மதிப்பீடு உண்மையான செயல்திறனை பிரதிபலிக்காது

en: Increases learning rate when loss plateaus
Reduces learning rate when a monitored metric stops improving
ta: Loss plateau ஆகும்போது learning rate-ஐ அதிகரிக்கிறது
கண்காணிக்கப்படும் metric மேம்படாதபோது learning rate-ஐ குறைக்கிறது
